structural_approach: Progressive enhancement inversion. The content was already in semantic HTML (sections, headings, paragraphs, a real data table). The accessibility failure was a single CSS pattern: .scene-inner defaulted to opacity:0 / translateY(28px), with reveal only via IntersectionObserver adding .is-visible. Without JS, every scene rendered blank to sighted users. The fix: invert the default — .scene-inner is fully visible by default. The hide-then-reveal pattern is scoped to html.js, a class set by a blocking inline script in <head> before <body> parses. JS users get the scroll-driven reveal with zero flash. Non-JS users, screen readers, and prefers-reduced-motion users get full linear reading order immediately.

changes.css_inversion: html.js .scene-inner { opacity: 0 } — hide is now a JS-gated progressive enhancement. Default is visible.
changes.js_guard: Inline <script> before </head> sets document.documentElement.classList.add('js') — blocks before body renders, so no FOUC for JS users.
changes.reduced_motion: html.js .scene-inner rule also gets @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override — honors both the preference AND the non-JS fallback.
changes.scene_1_heading:
Scene 1 had no heading element. Added

The board chair texted.

— H key navigation now covers all 9 scenes.
changes.answer_card_role_img: Removed role='img' from .answer-card-mock in Scene 4. That role collapsed a live <table> with pay equity numbers (IC5/IC6/M3 rows, €132k–€181k data), a SVG chart, and cite chips into a single AT-inaccessible label string. Assistive technology now navigates the actual content.
changes.q_pile_keyboard: Scene 7 question pile converted from <div class='q-card'> to <a class='q-card' href='../console/?q={slug}'> inside <li>. All six are now keyboard-reachable, focusable, and have meaningful href targets. :focus-visible added alongside :hover with 2px ochre outline.
changes.q_pile_semantics: .q-pile promoted to <ul> with list-style:none. Mobile responsive adds .q-pile li { display:contents } so <a> elements are the flex items.
changes.ink_3_aaa_token: --ink-3-aaa: #3d4456 added to :root. Previously only defined inside .site-nav scope — any use outside that scope (nav-links, outro cards, etc.) resolved to browser default.

no_separate_summary_page: The accessibility.html referenced a 'text summary' link as a planned workaround. That workaround was never shipped — no separate /tour/summary page exists. The inversion above makes the workaround unnecessary: the canonical page IS the linear reading experience. No anchor to a summary section was added; the in-page heading structure is the nav target.

wcag_verification.1.3.2_meaningful_sequence: Heading order is H1 (intro) -> H2 Scene 1 -> H2 Scene 2 -> H2 Scene 3 -> H2 Scene 4 -> H2 Scene 5 -> H2 Scene 6 -> H2 Scene 7 -> H2 Scene 8 -> H2 outro. Source order matches visual order. Passes.
wcag_verification.2.4.3_focus_order: All interactive elements in document order: skip link, site-nav, mobile-bar links, primary nav, play button (scene 0), scroll link, scene 7 q-cards (now <a>), outro cards, footer links, play-toggle, scene-pos (non-interactive). Passes.
wcag_verification.2.4.10_section_headings: Each of the 9 scenes now has a heading element. H key navigation covers the full tour. Passes.
wcag_verification.2.1.1_keyboard: q-pile cards were hover-only divs. Now <a> elements with href, :focus-visible styling, and visible focus ring (2px ochre outline). Passes.
wcag_verification.no_js_test: With JS disabled, every .scene-inner is visible at full opacity. Page reads Scene 0 -> 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> 5 -> 6 -> 7 -> 8 -> outro in document order. Passes.
wcag_verification.reduced_motion_test: html.js .scene-inner reduced-motion override disables opacity/transform animations even when JS is present. Auto-advance skips (existing prefersReducedMotion guard in play logic unchanged). Passes.

preserved: Scroll-driven IntersectionObserver reveal (for JS + no reduced-motion users)
console-frame role='img' in scenes 2 and 3 (those are decorative interface mockups, not live data)
